text:
The County of Yuba
Health and Human Services Department
TO: Board of Supervisors
FROM: Jennifer Vasquez, Director
Melissa Shaw, Deputy Director
Health & Human Services Department
SUBJECT: Adopt Resolution authorizing the Health and Human Services Department (HHSD)
Director to accept funds allocated by the California Department of Public Health for
the Maternal, Child and Adolescent Health program for fiscal year 2023-24 and
execute all related documents
DATE: March 12, 2024
NUMBER: 103/2024
Recommendation:
It is recommended that the Board of Supervisors:
1. Adopt the resolution of the Board authorizing the Director of Health and Human Services
Department (HHSD) to accept, transfer, and administer funds allocated by the California
Department of Public Health (CDPH) for the Maternal Child and Adolescent Health (MCAH)
program for the period of July 1, 2023 through June 30, 2024; and
2. Authorize the Director to execute all documents as required by the allocation and the resultant
grant; and to amend contracts for additional or lesser funding.
Background:
CDPH allocates MCAH funding to local health departments to provide proactive interventions,
resources and information about health topics that include reproductive health, pregnancy, child health,
and safe sleep options.
Discussion:
Since 2007, Yuba County has been allocated MCAH program funds to address the needs of new and
expectant mothers and the health and well-being of children and adolescents in Yuba County. Yuba

Page 2
County’s anticipated allocation for the period of July 1, 2023 through June 30, 2024 is $105,062. The
intended use of these funds will be for safe sleep educational materials and behavior modifiers with the
goal of improving the health and well-being of women, children and families. Personnel costs fund a
Public Health Nurse (PHN) position who maintains the role of MCAH Coordinator, Sudden Infant
Death Syndrome (SIDS) Coordinator, and Perinatal Services Coordinator (PSC). The PHN will be
completing hospital rounds to establish contact with new mothers at Adventist Health and Rideout
Hospital to share education on breastfeeding, safe sleep best practices, and support for postpartum
depression. For those new mothers that the PHN is not able to establish face-to-face contact with, an
informational packet will be mailed so that each new mother is provided support starting from the birth
of the child. In 2022, there were 1,846 births in Yuba County. Through this activity, Public Health will
establish a foundational relationship with each of those new mothers to provide proactive interventions
and support
Committee:
The Human Services Committee was bypassed due to the routine nature of this request and this item is
being placed on the Consent for Board of Supervisors approval.
Fiscal Impact:
Budget
 Board Approved FY 2023-24 Budget Appropriation
Total Projected Cost $105,062
Source of Funds
 Non-General Fund $105,062 State Funding CDPH
o MCAH $102.062
o SIDS $3,000
Attachment:
103/2024 Resolution for Maternal, Child and Adolescent Health Program
